task_completion.description: Open the image in the image editor (GIMP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is open with an image loaded on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Auto Normalize tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Colors' menu is open, navigating to 'Auto' and selecting 'Normalize'.

task_completion.description: Apply the normalization to improve contrast and tonal distribution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas shows a visible change in contrast, with darker darks and lighter lights, expanding the tonal range.

task_completion.description: Save or export the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save or export dialog is visible, or the 'File' menu is open with 'Overwrite' or 'Export' selected, indicating the changes are being saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the image editor to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent actually applied the normalization tool rather than replacing the file with a pre-edited version.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the image changing state within the GIMP canvas after the tool is applied.

integrity.description: The agent did not apply unrelated destructive filters or edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image content remains the same, only the contrast and tonal distribution are altered.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying GIMP's Auto Normalize function (Colors -> Auto -> Normalize). The verifier checks for dynamic range expansion (>=20%), contrast improvement (>=15%), minimal clipping (<2%), and meaningful modification. The original images have moderate-to-high contrast, so the normalization effect should visibly expand the tonal range without excessive clipping. A passing score requires meeting at least 3 out of 4 programmatic criteria.
